response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist software developer with broad web, mobile, and some systems experience, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. There is no indication of Japanese language proficiency, which is a hard requirement at native level. His C experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments, with no evidence of Linux kernel analysis or modification, embedded LAN-switch development, or the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal). His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms, which is a poor match for embedded firmware security work.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with some low-latency systems work (DLang, async I/O) and C/C++ exposure at National Instruments
Strong Linux, Docker, and CI/CD tooling background with demonstrated dev-ops and environment-building skills
Open-source contributor and community educator, suggesting initiative and ability to research new technologies
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed; native-level Japanese is a mandatory requirement
No experience with embedded networking, LAN switches, or the required security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, encryption/key exchange, tamper detection)
C for UNIX and Linux kernel source-modification experience is absent or far below the depth required
